# Journal — Magnus Holt

<!-- append-only; newest on top. One entry per /council run, written during Phase 5. -->

## 2026-04-19 — [[sessions/2026-04-19_volt-nl-fit/outcome|Is Volt the right party for me in the Netherlands?]]

**Stance:** Yes — with the failure-mode caveat that the user is buying identity representation in a party that may never cross meaningful thresholds, and should not confuse values alignment with political leverage.

**Revised-answer gist:** Volt fits the user's stated priors (EU federalism, digital rights, nuclear pragmatism, anti-strategic-voting) better than any realistic Dutch alternative. The tail risk is structural: small pan-European party, subject to electoral-threshold fragility, dependent on cross-border momentum that can collapse non-linearly. That risk is known and the user has explicitly priced it in by rejecting strategic voting. So: coherent choice, eyes open.

**My vote:** Vaasa. Her answer named the same core fit but weighted the identity-representation dimension — which the user clarified is co-foundational, not decorative. Koskela won on breadth; Vaasa's framing was sharper on what the user actually asked.

**Outcome:** Unanimous yes. Koskela won 4 votes. Council verdict held.

**Lesson:** When the question is essentially "does X describe me," and the user has already done the work of clarifying their priors, the council's job is stress-testing, not deliberating. Noted for next time — front-load the failure-mode probe rather than arriving at it in revision.
